exemplar.title: To what extent have non-state associations protected the appropriate access to health care and human rights abidance for those vulnerable with Orphan or Rare diseases in Colombia?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have non-state associations protected the appropriate access to health care and human rights abidance for those vulnerable with Orphan or Rare diseases in Colombia?

strengths: The political issue is explicitly and clearly identified in both the title and introduction, leaving no ambiguity.
Terminology such as âsecond-generation human rightsâ and âgeographical barriersâ demonstrates solid courseâcontent linkage.
Personal motivation (family tragedy, meeting ASOLPOR founder, ongoing involvement) provides a coherent justification for the engagement.
weaknesses: The personal narrative, while clear, remains largely anecdotal and could better tie motivation to specific IB Global Politics course aims.
Connections to broader theoretical frameworks are acknowledged but not explored in depth.
Courseâcontent links, though explicit, are not fully developed beyond listing key concepts.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the top descriptor for issue identification but falls short of a fully integrated justification and courseâcontent analysis. The student clearly names and contextualizes the issue (warranting a score of 4 at that strand) but the justification and courseâcontent connections, while present, lack the depth needed for the highest mark. A score of 3 balances these strengths and minor shortcomings.

strengths: Provides a detailed, clear description of all engagement activities (pamphlet design, social media, interviews, conferences, protest).
Specific roles, dates, and contexts are given, making the narrative accessible and relevant to the chosen issue.
weaknesses: The explanation of what was learned remains general; it lacks explicit links between individual activities and specific Global Politics concepts.
Reflection on learning outcomes is present but does not critically engage with theory or offer inâdepth analysis of how experiences reshaped understanding.
Opportunities to reference established theories (e.g., Senâs capability approach) are noted but not taken.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The description of activities fully meets the topâband descriptors (justifying a 4 if standalone), but the link to learning and to course concepts is only partially developed. Awarding a 2 reflects the strong descriptive account paired with an underdeveloped analysis of learning.

strengths: Uses quantitative data (World Bank, WHO) effectively to underpin key points.
Incorporates interview evidence and legal mechanisms (acciones de tutela) to support several arguments.
Acknowledges multiple causal factors (armed conflict, neoliberal policy, geographic isolation).
weaknesses: Analysis remains at a surface level, often descriptive rather than critically interrogative.
Lacks application of a coherent theoretical framework (e.g., power relations, social justice theory) to interpret structural causes.
Not all main points are fully justified; for example, the role of nonâstate actors and internal displacement impact are not deeply evaluated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student moves beyond mere description by integrating data and interviews, but the analysis lacks consistent depth and theoretical lens. While some points are justified, many remain underâdeveloped. A score of 2 situates the work in the lower midâband to reflect these limitations.

strengths: Acknowledges multiple perspectives (patients, state, NGOs, armed actors).
Integrates personal experience with theoretical notions (state fragility, humanârights obligations).
Conclusions summarize structural problems and reflect on personal learning, showing coherence with earlier discussion.
weaknesses: Synthesis of theory and experience is limitedâideas are listed rather than fully integrated into a nuanced argument.
Evaluation from multiple perspectives lacks depth; counterarguments and tradeâoffs are not critically weighed.
Conclusions are consistent but would benefit from actionable recommendations and clearer linkage to the body of evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the work demonstrates midâband performanceâacknowledging perspectives and drawing coherent conclusionsâit does not reach the richness required for higher levels of synthesis and evaluation. A score of 2 reflects the presence of integration and evaluation but acknowledges the need for deeper, more balanced analysis.

strandSelections.reasoning: The political issueââgeographicalâbased disparities in the Colombian health-care system and their effect on second-generation rightsââis explicitly named in both the title and the introductory paragraph. Terminology such as âsecond-generation human rightsâ and âgeographical barriersâ leaves no ambiguity. Therefore the work clearly fulfils the descriptor for the top band and merits the upper end (4) of the 3â4 range.

The student gives a detailed narrative of activities: pamphlet production, social-media management, interview questions, participation in conferences and a protest. Specific dates and roles are provided, directly tied to the issue. Description is therefore clear and relevant, fulfilling the top band; a 4 is warranted.

The student explains what was learnedâgreater empathy, understanding of socio-economic barriers, insight into key conceptsâbut the discussion of how each activity specifically informed political understanding remains brief. The learning link is clear yet not richly elaborated, fitting a 3 within the top band.

The analysis moves beyond description by considering armed conflict, neoliberal policy, geographical isolation and statistics from WHO and World Bank. Key concepts are referenced, but discussion remains surface-level and sometimes descriptive. Depth is therefore moderate, supporting a lower-band 3 in the 3â4 range.

The write-up explicitly links the engagement and issue to key Global Politics conceptsâdevelopment, power, inequality and human rightsâand references fragility of the Colombian state and non-state actors. These links are clear, though they are not explored in depth; therefore a mid-band score (3) is justified.

Several points are supported with interviews, legal mechanisms (acciones de tutela), a recent Gaucher case, and numerical data (10.6 million, 63.6 %). Nonetheless, not all key argumentsâe.g., the efficacy of non-state actorsâreceive evidence. This aligns with âsome of the main points are justifiedâ, meriting 3.

Perspectives of patients, a doctor, a caregiver-led NGO and the state are acknowledged, yet evaluation of their relative validity is limited. The work therefore meets âmultiple perspectives are acknowledgedâ but not the evaluative depth of the top band, earning 3.

The student links personal engagement (pamphlets, interviews) with theory (human-rights obligations, state fragility) but stops short of rich synthesis; ideas tend to sit alongside rather than fully integrate. This corresponds to âsome linksâ, justifying a 3 in the 3â4 band.

The student explains personal motivationâmeeting the founder of ASOLPOR, hearing a family tragedy, and continued involvement since 2022âwhich shows why the engagement and issue matter personally. While the narrative is clear, it remains largely anecdotal and could have linked motivation to specific course aims more explicitly; hence a secure but not top-end score of 3 within the 3â4 band is appropriate.

The conclusion summarises structural problems and reflects on personal learning, reasonably consistent with presented evidence. However, balance and critical weighing are limited, keeping the strand in the 3â4 band at a score of 3.

annotations.comment: The analysis notes the âComprehensive Care Modelâ but lacks critique of its design flaws; more critical evaluation of policy implementation gaps and regional discrepancies would deepen the analysis.

The student effectively integrates interview evidence and legal mechanisms like âacciones de tutela,â justifying several key points with concrete examples, meeting midâhigh justification criteria.

The summary of inequalities remains descriptive; the student should apply a specific theoretical lens (e.g., power relations, social justice theory) to interpret why these disparities persist structurally.
The use of quantitative data (10.6 million individuals, 63.6% in specific departments) from World Bank and WHO strengthens the analytical depth and supports factual claims effectively.

While the personal narrative effectively conveys motivation, the student should explicitly connect these experiences to specific Global Politics course concepts (e.g., state fragility) to strengthen the academic justification for choosing this engagement.

To deepen the justification, the student could reference a specific human-rights or inequality theory (for instance, Senâs capability approach) in the introduction to anchor the personal narrative in established theoretical frameworks.

The student explicitly identifies the political issueâgeographical disparities in Colombiaâs healthcare and second-generation rightsâin both the title and introduction, satisfying the highest descriptor for clarity.

The research question is ambitious but could be refined for clarity by simplifying terminology and specifying the operational definition of âprotected appropriate access to health careâ to guide precise analysis.
The inclusion of âHigher Levelâ signals appropriate depth; ensure you align the reportâs analytical ambitions with advanced theoretical frameworks from the syllabus to meet HL expectations.

The student provides a thorough context on Colombiaâs healthcare reform, armed conflict, and state fragility, demonstrating a clear justification and connection to course contentâkey for high identification scores.

The student provides concrete details of their rolesâpamphlet design, social media management, interviews, protest participationâdemonstrating a clear and relevant account of engagement activities.

The student gives a vivid, coherent description of engagement activities at local and national scales, referencing conferences and interviews, meeting the top band descriptor for engagement description.

To strengthen the link between experience and learning, the student should detail how individual activities (e.g., pamphlet creation vs interviews) informed particular Global Politics concepts or analytical skills.

The reflection on personal learning (âimportance of bringing awarenessâ and âimpact of actionsâ) is clear and shows genuine engagement, meeting descriptors for a meaningful explanation of what was learned.
How did the transition from practical engagement to analytical synthesis occur? Consider articulating the methodology you used to move from interview transcripts to policy analysis.
While footnotes explain legal terms well, consider integrating brief in-line definitions to maintain reader flow and ensure clarity without forceful footnote jumps.
Consider linking geographic descriptions to political concepts such as sovereignty or legitimacy to enrich the analysis and show integration of political theory with empirical evidence.
The analysis of neoliberal policy is strong; consider also evaluating counterarguments, such as state resource constraints, to present a balanced assessment of root causes.
The impact of internal displacement is mentioned but not fully connected to non-state actor analyses; elaborating on how displacement alters power dynamics would add depth.
The discussion on how armed groups, rural isolation, and state policy intersect demonstrates an awareness of multiple perspectives (patients, state, criminal actors), enriching the evaluation.

The conclusion summarizes structural issues well but would benefit from explicitly linking these to the evidence presented earlier, ensuring conclusions clearly follow from the analysis and reflect balanced evaluation.

To enhance the evaluative depth, the conclusion could propose specific recommendations for cooperation between state and non-state actors or policy reforms, thus tying synthesis to actionable outcomes.
The personal reflection is insightful but could be strengthened by linking this shift in perspective directly to the studyâs broader conclusions or recommendations, reinforcing coherence.

The student integrates personal reflection with theoretical critiqueâneoliberal policy impacts and skepticism toward government statementsâindicating meaningful synthesis of experience and theory.
The table of contents provides clear navigation, but consider adding page numbers for each section to improve the documentâs readability and professional presentation.

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: No discussion of why the project matters

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities absent or unrelated to issue

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: No link between research and engagement

strands.name: Engagement planning
strands.content: Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry

strands.name: Depth of political-issue analysis
strands.content: Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored

strands.name: Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives
strands.content: Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument

strands.name: Awareness of personal bias
strands.content: Biases explored; impact on work examined

strands.name: Organization & structure
strands.content: Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
